note_data.title: Writing skills
keyPoints: Identify **audience** and **purpose** clearly before writing to ensure appropriate tone and content
Use **varied vocabulary** including sophisticated words, idiomatic expressions and technical terms when appropriate
Apply **Standard English grammar** with accurate tenses, subject-verb agreement and sentence construction
Structure writing with clear **introduction, development and conclusion** using appropriate paragraphs
Create a detailed **plan** showing main points and supporting details before writing
Adopt consistent **voice** (formal/informal) that matches audience and purpose throughout
Use **discourse markers** (however, furthermore, consequently) to link ideas coherently
Apply **punctuation accurately** including complex punctuation for effect and clarity

definitions.definition: The specific group of people for whom the writing is intended, affecting choice of vocabulary, tone and content
The reason for writing such as to inform, persuade, entertain, advise or describe
The consistent tone and style adopted throughout the writing that reflects the relationship between writer and reader
The level of formality in language that matches the audience and purpose
Words and phrases that connect ideas and show relationships between sentences and paragraphs

howToAnswer.instruction: Produce the specified text type ensuring correct format, audience awareness and purpose
Show clear structure with main points and supporting details before writing

modelAnswer.question: Write the opening paragraph of a letter to your school principal suggesting improvements to the library facilities. (6 marks)
modelAnswer.answer:
Dear Principal Ahmed,

I am writing to respectfully suggest several improvements to our school library that would significantly enhance the learning experience for all students. As a dedicated student who frequently uses the library for research and study, I have observed certain areas where thoughtful enhancements could transform this vital educational resource into an even more effective learning environment that meets the diverse needs of our student body.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Appropriate respectful tone for audience
Mark 6: Establishes credibility and engages reader

commonMistakes: Never use informal language like 'gonna' or 'cos' in formal writing — always use Standard English
Students often forget to plan and produce unstructured writing with poor paragraph organization
Never use the same simple vocabulary throughout — always vary word choices and use sophisticated alternatives
Students frequently ignore audience and use inappropriate tone for the intended reader
Never write without clear purpose — always ensure every paragraph serves the writing objective
examinerTips: Writing tasks appear in both Paper 1 and Paper 2 with different formats and requirements
Always spend 5 minutes planning before writing to ensure clear structure and content
Read the question twice to identify exact audience, purpose and format requirements
Use the full range of punctuation including semicolons and colons for higher marks
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Planning framework showing: Audience identification → Purpose clarification → Structure outline (introduction/development/conclusion) → Key points with supporting details → Vocabulary and tone decisions
